L1.area: Login page rendering and authentication flow UI layer

L2.description: A cross-site scripting (XSS) and code injection vulnerability exists where user-controlled parameters sent via a GET request during the login process are reflected into the rendered page output without proper escaping. This allows an attacker to inject arbitrary scripts and platform-specific syntax from the login screen, enabling remote code execution via interpreted macro languages. The vulnerability affects the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of the entire installation.

L3.area: OAuth login page UI extension and the Velocity template macros used for rendering translated content
L3.description: A cross-site scripting (XSS) and code injection vulnerability exists in the OAuth login flow where the identity provider operation parameter, sent via a GET request, is interpolated into an HTML page without proper output escaping. Additionally, shared Velocity template macros that render localized translation strings output their results without sanitization. This allows injection of arbitrary scripts and platform-specific syntax from the login screen, enabling remote code execution via interpreted macro languages.

ground_truth.reason: User-controlled OAuth GET request parameters are reflected into HTML output without escaping, allowing XSS and XWiki syntax injection that enables remote code execution via Groovy macros.
